volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 2.3.389b - Section 3.2.487)

sections.label: Section 2.3.389b
sections.original: Certo , tibi et parasito tuo .
sections.translation: Certainly you did , for yourself and your Parasite .

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.original: Cui , malum , parasito ? certo haec mulier non sanast satis .
sections.translation: A plague , what Parasite ? Surely this woman isn ’ t quite right in her senses .

sections.label: Section 2.3.391
sections.original: Peniculo .
sections.translation: Peniculus .

sections.label: Section 2.3.391b
sections.original: Quis iste ést Peniculus ? qui extergentur baxeae ?
sections.translation: Who is this Peniculus The one with which the shoes are wiped clean [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.392
sections.original: Scilicet qui dudum tecum venit , quom pallam mihi
sections.translation: Him , I mean , who came with you a while ago , when you brought me the mantle which you purloined from your wife .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What do you mean ? I , gave you a mantle , which I purloined from my wife ? Are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.original: certe haec mulier cantherino ritu ástans somniat .
sections.translation: Surely this woman dreams standing , after the manner of a gelding [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.3.396
sections.original: Qui lubet ludibrio habere me atque ire infitias mihi
sections.translation: Why does it please you to hold me in ridicule , and to deny to me things that have been done by you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.397b
sections.original: Dic quid est id quod negem quod fecerim ?
sections.translation: Tell me what it is that I deny after having done it ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.398
sections.original: Pallam te hodie mihi dedisse uxoris .
sections.translation: That you to-day gave me your wife ’ s mantle .

sections.label: Section 2.3.398b
sections.original: Etiam nunc nego .
sections.translation: Even still do I deny it . Indeed , I never had a wife , nor have I one ; nor

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: umquam , postquam natus sum , intra portam penetravi pedem .
sections.translation: have I ever set my foot here within the city gate since I was born . I breakfasted on board ship ; thence did I come this way , and here I met you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.401b
sections.original: Eccere ,
sections.translation: See that now ; I ’ m undone , wretched creature that I am ! What ship are you now telling me about ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.402b
sections.original: Ligneam ,
sections.translation: A wooden one , weather-beaten full oft , cracked full oft , many a time thumped with mallets . Just as the implements of the furrier [*] ; so peg is close to peg .

sections.label: Section 2.3.405
sections.original: Iám , amabo , desiste ludos facere atque i hac mecum semul .
sections.translation: Now , prithee , do leave off making fun of me , and step this way with me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.406
sections.original: Nescio quem , mulier , alium hóminem , non me quaeritas .
sections.translation: ⋯ for , madam , you are looking for some other person , I know not whom , not me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.407
sections.original: Non ego te novi Menaechmum , Moscho prognatum patre ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t I know you , Menaechmus , the son of your father Moschus , who are said to have been born in Sicily , at Syracuse , where King Agathocles reigned , and after him Pintia [*] , the third Liparo , who at his death left the kingdom to Hiero — which Hiero is now king ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.412b
sections.original: Haud falsa , mulier , praedicas .
sections.translation: You say , madam , what is not untrue .

sections.label: Section 2.3.412c
sections.original: Pro Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: By Jupiter , hasn ’ t this woman come from there , who knows you so readily ? ⋯

sections.label: Section 2.3.414_415
sections.original: Hercle opinor , pernegari non potest .
sections.translation: Troth , I think she must not be denied .

sections.label: Section 2.3.414_415b
sections.original: Ne feceris . periisti , si intrassis intra limen .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you do it . You are undone , if you enter inside her threshold .

sections.label: Section 2.3.416b
sections.original: Quin tu tace modo .
sections.translation: But you only hold your tongue ⋯ The matter goes on well . I shall assent to the woman , whatever she shall say , if I can get some entertainment . Just now , madam ( speaking to her in a low voice ) ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.419_420
sections.original: non imprudens advorsabar : hunc metuebam , ne meae
sections.translation: I contradicted you not undesignedly ; I was afraid of that fellow , lest he might carry word to my wife about the mantle and the breakfast . Now , when you please , let ’ s go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.422b
sections.original: Etiam parasitum manes ?
sections.translation: Are you going to wait for the Parasite as well ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.423
sections.original: Neque ego illum maneo , neque flocci facio , neque , si venerit ,
sections.translation: I ’ m neither going to wait for him , nor do I care a straw for him , nor , if he should come , do I want him to be admitted in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.424b
sections.original: Ecastor haud invita fecero .
sections.translation: By my faith , I shall do that not at all reluctantly . But do you know what I beg you to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.425b
sections.original: Impera quid vis modo .
sections.translation: Only command me what you will .

sections.label: Section 2.3.426
sections.original: Pallam illam , quam dudum dederas , ad phrygionem ut deferas ,
sections.translation: For you to take that mantle which you gave me just now to the embroiderer ’ s [*] , that it may be trimmed again , and that some work may be added which I want .

sections.label: Section 2.3.428
sections.original: Hercle qui tu recte dicis : eádem ignorabitur ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you say what ’ s right ; in such a way shall it be disguised that my wife shan ’ t know that you are wearing it , if she should see you in the street .

sections.label: Section 2.3.430
sections.original: Ergo mox auferto tecum , quando abibis .
sections.translation: Then take it away with you just now , when you go away .

sections.label: Section 2.3.430b
sections.original: Maxime .
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 2.3.431
sections.original: Eamus intro
sections.translation: Let ’ s go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.431b
sections.original: Iam sequar te . húnc volo etiam conloqui .
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow you this instant ; I only wish to speak to this person . So , there ! Messenio , step to me this way .

sections.label: Section 2.3.432b
sections.original: Quid negoti est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.432c
sections.original: † sussciri .
sections.translation: Listen .

sections.label: Section 2.3.433
sections.original: Quid eo opust ?
sections.translation: What need for it ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.433b
sections.original: Opus est . scio ut me dices .
sections.translation: There is need , I know what you ’ ll say to me —

sections.label: Section 2.3.433c
sections.original: Tanto nequior .
sections.translation: So much the worse .

sections.label: Section 2.3.434_435
sections.original: Habeo praedam : tantum incepi óperis . ei quantum potes ,
sections.translation: Hold your tongue ⋯ I ’ ve got some spoil ; thus much of the business have I begun upon . Go , and , as quick as you can , take away those people [*] at once to an inn [*] . Then do you take care to come and meet me [*] before sunset .

sections.label: Section 2.3.438
sections.original: Non tu istas meretrices novisti , ere .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know that these people are harlots , master ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.438b
sections.original: Tace , inquam ⋯
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , I say , and go you away from here . It will cost me pain , not you , if I do anything here that ’ s foolish .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440
sections.original: mulier haec stulta atque inscita est ; quantum perspexi modo ,
sections.translation: This woman is silly and inexperienced . So far as I ’ ve perceived just now , there ’ s some spoil for us here .

sections.label: Section 2.3.441b
sections.original: Perii , iamne abis ? periit probe :
sections.translation: I ’ m undone . Are you going away then ? He is certainly ruined ; the piratical craft is now leading the boat straight to destruction . But I ’ m an unreasonable fellow to wish to rule my master ; he bought me to obey his orders , not to be his commander .

sections.label: Section 2.3.445
sections.original: sequimini , ut , quod imperatum est , veniam advorsum temperi .—
sections.translation: ( To the ATTENDANTS .) Follow me , that , as I ’ m ordered , I may come in good time to meet my master .

sections.label: Section 3.1.446
sections.original: Plus triginta ánnis natus sum , quom ínterea loci ,
sections.translation: More than thirty years have I been born yet during that time I never did any more mischievous or more evil trick than this day , when , to my misfortune , I thrust myself into the midst of the assembly [*] . while I was gaping about there , Menaechmus stole away from me ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.450
sections.original: atque abiit ad amicam , credo , neque me voluit ducere .
sections.translation: and went , I suppose , to his mistress , and didn ’ t want to take me . May all the Divinities confound that man who first mischievously devised the holding of an assembly , which keeps men thus engaged . By my troth , is it not fitting that men who are disengaged should be chosen for that purpose ? These , when they are cited , if they are not present , let the officers exact the fine [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.455
sections.translation: forthwith ⋯ the senate ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.1.457
sections.original: adfatim est hominum , in dies qui singulas escas edint ,
sections.translation: Abundance of men are there who every day eat their victuals alone , who have no business , who are neither invited nor invite to feast ; these ought to give their attendance at the assembly and the law-courts [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.460
sections.original: sí id ita esset , non ego hodie perdidissem prandium ,
sections.translation: If so it had been , this say I shouldn ’ t have lost my breakfast ; to which I deemed myself as much accustomed , as to see myself alive . I ’ ll go ; even yet the hope of the scraps comforts my mind . But why do I see Menaechmus here ? He ’ s coming out of doors with a chaplet on ? The banquet is removed ; i ’ faith , I come just in time to meet him .

sections.label: Section 3.1.465
sections.original: observabo , quid agat , hominem . post adibo atque adloquar .
sections.translation: I ’ ll watch the fellow , what he ’ s about , then I ’ ll go and accost him . ( He steps aside .)

sections.label: Section 3.2.466
sections.original: Potine ut quiescas ? ego tibi hanc hodie probe
sections.translation: Can ’ t you rest content , if this day I bring it you back in good time , nicely and properly trimmed ? I ’ ll cause you to say it isn ’ t itself , so much shall it be disguised .

sections.label: Section 3.2.469
sections.original: Pallam ad phrygionem fert confecto prandio
sections.translation: He ’ s carrying the mantle to the embroiderer ’ s , the breakfast finished

sections.label: Section 3.2.470
sections.original: vinoque expoto , parasito excluso foras .
sections.translation: and the wine drunk up , and the Parasite shut out of doors . By my troth , I ’ m not the person that I am , if I don ’ t handsomely avenge this injury and myself . ’ Tis requisite I should watch ⋯ I ’ ll give something .

sections.label: Section 3.2.473
sections.original: Pro di immortales , quoí homini umquam uno die
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods ! on what man ever have you conferred more blessings in one day , who hoped for less ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.475
sections.original: prandi , potavi , scortum accubui , apstuli
sections.translation: I ’ ve been breakfasting , drinking , feasting with a mistress ; and I ’ ve carried off this mantle , of which she shall no more be owner after this day .

sections.label: Section 3.2.477
sections.original: Nequeo quae loquitur exaudire clanculum ;
sections.translation: Isn ’ t he now talking about me , and my share of the repast ? I can ’ t well hear what he says .

sections.label: Section 3.2.479_480
sections.original: Ait hanc dedisse me sibí , atque eam meae
sections.translation: She says that I secretly gave her this , and that I stole it away from my wife . When I perceived that she was mistaken , at once I began to assent , as though I really had had acquaintanceship with her . Whatever the woman said , the same said I . What need of many words ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.485
sections.original: minore nusquam bene fui dispendio .
sections.translation: I was never entertained at less expense .

sections.label: Section 3.2.486
sections.original: Adibo ad hominem , nam turbare gestio .
sections.translation: I ’ ll accost the fellow ; for I quite long to have a row .

sections.label: Section 3.2.487
sections.original: Quis hic ést , qui adversus it mihi ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s this that ’ s coming up towards me ? ( Takes off the mantle , and hides it .)
